title: New Python Interview Q&A Sheet
content:
■ Python Interview

Questions & Answers

Top 15 Frequently Asked Python Interview Questions with Clear, Concise Answers

1. What is Python? What are the benefits of using Python?

Answer:
Python is a high-level, interpreted, and general-purpose programming language known for its simple and readable syntax. It was created by Guido van Rossum.

Benefits:

  • Easy to learn and use

  • Large standard library

  • Cross-platform support

  • Open-source and free

  • Used in Web Development, AI, Data Science, and Automation

2. What is a Dynamically Typed Language?

Answer:
A dynamically typed language is one in which the data type of a variable is determined at runtime. We do not need to declare the variable type explicitly.

Example:

x = 10
x = "Hello"

3. What is an Interpreted Language?

Answer:
An interpreted language executes code line by line using an interpreter instead of compiling the entire program before execution.

Python is an interpreted language, which makes debugging easier and development faster.

4. What is PEP 8 and why is it important?

Answer:
PEP 8 (Python Enhancement Proposal 8) is the official coding style guide for Python.

It is important because:

  • Improves code readability

  • Maintains consistency

  • Makes collaboration easier

  • Helps write clean and professional code

5. What are the common built-in data types in Python?

Answer:
Some common built-in data types in Python are:

  • int → Integer values

  • float → Decimal values

  • str → Text/String values

  • bool → True or False

  • list → Ordered collection

  • tuple → Immutable collection

  • set → Unordered unique elements

  • dict → Key-value pairs

6. Explain the Ternary Operator in Python.

Answer:
The ternary operator is a one-line shortcut for an if-else statement.

Syntax:

value_if_true if condition else value_if_false

Example:

result = "Pass" if marks >= 40 else "Fail"

It makes code shorter and more readable.

7. What does the is operator do?

Answer:
The is operator checks whether two variables refer to the same object in memory.

Example:

a = [1,2]
b = a

print(a is b)

Output:

True

8. What are the disadvantages of Python?

Answer:
Some disadvantages of Python are:

  • Slower execution compared to C/C++

  • High memory consumption

  • Not ideal for mobile application development

  • Less suitable for low-level programming

9. How are strings stored in Python?

Answer:
Strings are stored as a sequence of Unicode characters. Each character has an index position.

Example:

s = "Hello"

Python stores strings as immutable objects, meaning they cannot be modified after creation.

10. What is the Zen of Python?

Answer:
The Zen of Python is a collection of principles that guide Python programmers to write clean and readable code.

It can be displayed using:

import this

One famous principle is:

"Simple is better than complex."

11. Difference between is and ==?

Answer:

is

==

Checks object identity

Checks value equality

Same memory location

Same content/value

Example:

a = [1,2]
b = [1,2]

print(a == b)  # True
print(a is b)  # False

12. What does _ variable represent in Python?

Answer:
The underscore (_) is commonly used as a placeholder variable when a value is not needed.

Example:

x, _, z = (1,2,3)

Here, the value 2 is ignored.

It is also used as a temporary variable in loops and interactive sessions.

13. Difference between Module, Package, and Library?

Answer:

Module

A single Python file containing code.

Example:

math.py

Package

A collection of related modules stored in a folder.

Library

A collection of packages and modules that provide specific functionality.

Example: NumPy, Pandas

14. Why is 0.3 - 0.2 not exactly equal to 0.1 in Python?

Answer:
Computers store decimal numbers in binary format. Some decimal values cannot be represented exactly in binary, which causes small precision errors.

Example:

print(0.3 - 0.2)

Output:

0.09999999999999998

This is known as floating-point precision error.

15. What are Python Docstrings?

Answer:
Docstrings are special strings used to describe the purpose of modules, classes, and functions.

They are written inside triple quotes (""" """).

Example:

def add(a, b):
    """Returns the sum of two numbers."""
    return a + b

Advantages:

  • Improves code documentation

  • Makes code easier to understand

  • Helps generate automatic documentation

Interview Tip

For most Python interviews, keep answers in this format:

Definition → Example → Advantage/Use Case

This structure sounds professional and is easy for interviewers to follow.

versions.title: Initial version
versions.content:
■ Python Interview

Questions & Answers

Top 15 Frequently Asked Python Interview Questions with Clear, Concise Answers

Q1. What is Python? What are the benefits of using Python?

Python is a high-level, general-purpose, interpreted programming language created by Guido van Rossum in 1991. It emphasizes code readability and simplicity.

Benefits:

  • Easy to learn & read — clean, English-like syntax
  • Versatile — used in web dev, data science, AI/ML, automation, scripting
  • Large standard library — 'batteries included' philosophy
  • Huge community & ecosystem — millions of third-party packages (PyPI)
  • Cross-platform — runs on Windows, macOS, Linux
  • Rapid prototyping — fewer lines of code compared to Java/C++
  • Dynamically typed — no need to declare variable types explicitly
Q2. What is a Dynamically Typed Language?

In a dynamically typed language, variable types are determined at runtime, not at compile time. You don't need to declare a variable's type — Python figures it out automatically.

x = 10  # x is int
x = "hello"  # now x is str - no error!
x = [1, 2, 3]  # now x is list - perfectly valid
■ The type is bound to the VALUE, not the VARIABLE. Contrast this with Java/C++ where int x = 10; and then x = "hello" causes a compile-time error.
Q3. What is an Interpreted Language?

An interpreted language executes code line by line at runtime using an interpreter, rather than compiling the entire program to machine code beforehand.

How Python works:

  • Source code (.py) ➔ Python Interpreter ➔ Bytecode (.pyc) ➔ PVM (Python Virtual Machine) ➔ Output

Advantages: Easier debugging, platform independence, interactive shell (REPL)
